title: STARTELEMENT
text: STARTELEMENT Starts a new element using the name, including optional prefix and URI elements. Explain this code STARTELEMENT (Prefix,ElementName,URI) Copy Parameters Table 1. STARTELEMENT parameters Parameter Data Type Direction Description Prefix String IN Namespace ID prefixed to the ElementName and URI elements, if they are specified; maximum length is 40 bytes. ElementName String IN Element name; mandatory; maximum length is 255 bytes. URI String IN URL or other URI for the namespace; maximum length is 512 bytes. Return Values Values returned: 0 Success. –1 Failure. Called in an illegal sequence. Description The STARTELEMENT operation inserts the beginning of an element tag. The closing bracket of the open element tag ( > ) is added by subsequent operation calls. The UXMLWRITER component ensures that the result is legal XML, so you need not concern yourself with this. It is indented with spaces if it is a nested element. The STARTELEMENT can specify a namespace using the Prefix and URI. To specy additional namespaces, use the ADDPREFIXMAPPING operation. This operation must follow a call to STARTDOCUMENT , ENDELEMENT (to close a previous element), STARTELEMENT (to create an element nested inside another), or DOCCOMMENT . Assuming that uxm1 is an instance of UXMLWRITER, the following examples insert the indicated text into the XML document managed by uxml1 , indented with spaces if it is a nested element: Add <Aaa2 : Explain this code uxml1->startElement("", "Aaa2", "") Copy Add <hoi:Aaa1 : Explain this code uxml1->startElement("hoi", "Aaa1", "a/b/c") Copy Add <hoi:Aaa1 : Explain this code uxml1->startElement("hoi", "Aaa1", "") Copy Add <Aaa1 xmlns="a/b/c" : Explain this code uxml1->startElement("", "Aaa1", "a/b/c") Copy Related Links ADDPREFIXMAPPING Adds a namespace to the beginning of an element tag.
